marketFocus: Iran War Week 2: Oil surged to $120/bbl as UAE & Kuwait cut production, Iraq output down 60% to 1.7-1.8mln bpd; G7 emergency meeting to discuss joint release of up to 300-400mln barrels of emergency reserves; WTI settled $90.90/bbl Fri, surged further Mon open

NFP Aftermath + Fed on Hold: Feb NFP -92k (exp. 59k, prev. 126k), Unemployment Rate 4.4% (prev. 4.3%), wages sticky/hot — Fed speakers Hammack, Collins, Schmid all signal 'on hold for some time'; Bowman still supports 75bps cuts; Miran (voter) wants cut at next FOMC

China CPI Beat cushions AUD/NZD: China CPI Feb +1.3% YoY (exp. 0.8%, prev. 0.2%) — significant beat; PBoC mid-point 6.9158; Trump-Xi summit unlikely to yield breakthrough per Reuters; German IP Jan -0.5% MoM (exp. +0.9%) — miss adds to EUR stagflation narrative

USD.summary: DXY surged on a haven bid as the Iran conflict entered its second week and oil prices spiked to near $120/bbl. Despite the NFP miss (-92k), the combination of geopolitical risk premium, sticky wages, and Fed on-hold signals (Hammack, Collins) keeps the dollar bid. US Daylight Saving Time shift means London-NY spread is now 4 hours — watch for liquidity adjustments. DXY remains the cleanest haven in G10 with no near-term catalyst to reverse.
drivers: DXY haven surge — Iran war week 2, oil near $120/bbl
NFP -92k miss but wages sticky/hot — stagflation risk supports dollar
Fed on hold: Hammack, Collins, Schmid all signal patience
US Daylight Saving Time shift — London-NY spread now 4 hours
No domestic data today — geopolitics and oil narrative dominate

EUR.summary: EUR/USD retreated below 1.1600 as energy disruption roiled European markets. German Industrial Production Jan -0.5% MoM (exp. +0.9%) — a significant miss adding to the stagflation narrative. Euro Stoxx 50 futures down 2.1% at Monday open. ECB speakers Elderson and Cipollone due today — any dovish signal would accelerate EUR weakness. EUR is caught between rising energy costs (LNG, nat gas) and slowing growth, with no near-term catalyst to reverse.
drivers: EUR/USD below 1.1600 — energy shock, risk-off pressure
German IP Jan -0.5% MoM (exp. +0.9%) — miss deepens stagflation narrative
Euro Stoxx 50 futures -2.1% — European equity selloff
ECB Elderson, Cipollone speak today — watch for dovish signals
Energy importer: LNG disruption + nat gas surge = structural headwind

GBP.summary: GBP/USD briefly dipped below 1.3300 as Trump publicly criticised the UK for lack of support in Iran strikes, saying 'the US does not need UK aircraft carriers but will remember.' UK is a net energy importer — Iran war = stagflationary headwind. One UK aircraft carrier placed on advanced readiness. BoE cut path remains intact (Goldman Sachs: April). Daily Bearish mismatches Weekly Weak Bullish — no High Conviction alignment. GBP underperforms in this environment.
drivers: GBP/USD dipped below 1.3300 — Trump criticism of UK, risk-off
UK energy importer: Iran war = stagflationary headwind for GBP
UK aircraft carrier on advanced readiness — geopolitical risk premium
BoE cut path intact: Goldman Sachs April cut expected
No UK domestic data today — entirely geopolitics-driven

JPY.summary: USD/JPY gapped above 158.00 at Monday open as the dollar reigned supreme. Despite firmer-than-expected Japanese wage data (Average Cash Earnings beat), JPY failed to attract haven flows — the dollar dominated. Japan Chief Cabinet Secretary Kihara said they will 'closely monitor market trends with high urgency.' Japan is a 90%+ oil importer — the oil surge is deeply stagflationary for Japan. BoJ rate hike probability for March remains very low. JPY is the weakest haven in G10 in this environment.
drivers: USD/JPY gapped above 158.00 — dollar dominance, JPY haven failure
Japan Average Cash Earnings beat but JPY still sold — dollar wins
Japan CCS Kihara: monitoring markets with high urgency
Japan 90%+ oil importer — WTI $90-120 = severe stagflation risk
BoJ March hike probability near zero — policy divergence vs Fed

AUD.tone: China Beat vs Risk-Off Tug of War
AUD.summary: AUD caught between two opposing forces: China CPI Feb +1.3% YoY (exp. 0.8%, prev. 0.2%) — a significant beat that cushioned Antipodean downside — versus the broad risk-off environment with ASX 200 slumping across all sectors except energy. Oil price surge adds hawkish pressure to RBA outlook (outside bets for March hike emerged). Westpac Consumer Confidence (Mar) due today. AUD is not cleanly directional — Weekly Neutral confirmed. No High Conviction alignment possible.
drivers: China CPI Feb +1.3% YoY (exp. 0.8%) — beat cushions AUD downside
ASX 200 slumped at open — all sectors down except energy
Oil surge adds hawkish RBA optionality — March hike outside bet
Westpac Consumer Confidence Mar due today — watch for sentiment
Weekly Neutral — no High Conviction alignment

CAD.summary: CAD is the standout G10 performer in this environment. WTI settled $90.90/bbl Friday and surged further Monday — directly benefiting Canada as a major oil exporter. PMT US FX Wrap explicitly noted 'Crude rally supports CAD strength.' Canada-EU trade deal modernisation signed. BoC hawkish stance (Kozicki) intact. Canada Employment data due Friday. CAD is the cleanest long in G10 against bearish currencies in the current oil-shock environment.
drivers: WTI $90.90/bbl Fri, surged further Mon — direct CAD terms-of-trade positive
PMT FX Wrap: 'Crude rally supports CAD strength' — explicit confirmation
Canada-EU trade deal modernisation signed — positive structural backdrop
BoC hawkish stance intact — rates divergence vs ECB/BoE
CAD Employment + Unemployment Rate due Friday 13:30 Frankfurt

CHF.summary: CHF remains the premier G10 haven, outperforming even JPY in the current risk-off environment. SNB sight deposits rising — intervention monitoring active. Switzerland's energy independence and low inflation make CHF the cleanest safe haven. SNB March 19 meeting is the key near-term event — hot Swiss CPI Feb (+0.6% MoM) reduces probability of a cut. Morgan Stanley targets EUR/CHF at 0.87. CHF is the go-to haven in any escalation scenario.
drivers: Premier haven: outperforms JPY, SNB sight deposits rising
Switzerland energy independent — Iran war = no direct stagflation hit
Swiss CPI Feb +0.6% MoM (hot) — reduces SNB March 19 cut probability
Morgan Stanley: EUR/CHF target 0.87 — structural CHF strength
SNB March 19 meeting — key near-term event for CHF direction

NZD.summary: NZD is the most vulnerable G10 currency in the current environment. Despite China CPI beat (+1.3% YoY) providing some cushion, NZD underperforms even AUD as carry unwinds and risk-off dominates. RBNZ dovish pivot under Governor Breman creates a domestic rates handicap. NZD is typically the first currency cut in risk-off episodes. No NZD domestic data today — entirely driven by China headlines and global risk sentiment. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = aligned for shorts.
drivers: Most vulnerable G10 — first cut in risk-off, carry unwind target
RBNZ dovish pivot: OCR at current levels near term
China CPI beat (+1.3%) cushions but does not reverse NZD weakness
No domestic data today — entirely sentiment and China driven
Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = aligned for NZD shorts

bonds.name: US 10Y Treasury
bonds.summary: 10yr UST futures wiped out the gains seen following Friday's disappointing NFP data, as the surge in oil prices stoked inflationary fears. Energy prices continue to rise — ECB rate hike probability by year-end now elevated. Fed likely to hold in March/May. Oil-driven inflation = bond negative. Watch CPI Wednesday as the key catalyst for yield direction this week.
drivers: 10yr UST futures sold — oil surge stokes inflation fears
NFP -92k miss initially bid bonds, but oil reversal overwhelmed
Fed on hold: Hammack, Collins, Schmid all signal patience
US CPI Wednesday 18:30 Frankfurt — key catalyst for yield direction
Oil at $90-120/bbl = persistent inflation premium in yields

bonds.name: German Bund 10Y
bonds.summary: Bund futures fell firmly below 127.00 as oil prices rocketed. German IP Jan -0.5% MoM (exp. +0.9%) — stagflation narrative reinforced. ECB speakers Elderson and Cipollone due today. Energy-driven inflation reduces ECB's ability to cut rates despite slowing growth — Bunds face dual headwind of higher inflation and weaker growth.
drivers: Bund futures below 127.00 — oil surge, stagflation narrative
German IP Jan -0.5% MoM (exp. +0.9%) — miss adds to stagflation
ECB Elderson, Cipollone speak today — watch for policy signals
Energy-driven inflation limits ECB easing — Bund negative
Euro Stoxx 50 futures -2.1% — equity selloff adds to risk-off yield pressure

bonds.name: JGB 10Y
bonds.summary: 10yr JGB futures conformed to the downside in global peers with yields climbing on inflationary pressures. Japanese wages topped forecasts — BoJ hike optionality intact but March probability near zero. Japan is a 90%+ oil importer — oil at $90-120/bbl is deeply stagflationary for Japan. JGB yields rising despite BoJ's cautious stance.
drivers: JGB futures lower — conforms to global bond selloff
Japanese wages beat — BoJ hike optionality intact but March unlikely
Japan 90%+ oil importer — oil surge = severe stagflation risk
Japan Final GDP Q4 due today — watch for growth confirmation
Japan CCS Kihara: monitoring markets with high urgency
